channel.title: APGO
channel.description: World affairs, economics, technology, history, ideas and football analysis.

item.title: Renewed strikes put commercial shipping at risk in the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The IMO had recorded 58 confirmed regional maritime incidents and 17 seafarer deaths by 17 July. Another 6,000 seafarers were stranded aboard vessels unable to leave the Gulf safely.

item.title: NATO commits to two years of Ukraine support at Ankara summit
item.description: Alliance leaders pledged €70 billion in military support for 2026 and at least the same level in 2027 as UN monitors recorded June’s highest monthly civilian toll since April 2022.

item.title: Governments face a costly wave of debt refinancing
item.description: The OECD expects governments and companies to borrow $29 trillion from bond markets in 2026. Refinancing accounts for 78 per cent of projected borrowing by OECD governments.

item.title: Data-centre demand is testing power grids and utility forecasts
item.description: The IEA projects global data-centre electricity use could nearly double by 2030. Utilities must decide who pays for new capacity when large customer forecasts prove wrong.

item.title: How printers and riders carried the Declaration across the colonies
item.description: The Declaration reached the public through broadsides, newspapers and public readings. Its distribution in 1776 shows how a political text acquired force through circulation.

item.title: The Sun Also Rises in its 100th year: expatriates, war and Pamplona
item.description: Published on 22 October 1926, Hemingway’s first novel will reach its centenary this autumn. Its routes through Paris and Pamplona still shape the expatriate myth.

item.title: Spain beat Argentina in extra time to win the 2026 World Cup
item.description: Ferran Torres scored in the 106th minute of a 1–0 final. Spain conceded one goal in eight matches and won its second men’s World Cup.

item.title: Dostoevsky’s Underground Man, resentment and the imagined audience
item.description: The 1864 narrator invents hostile readers, rehearses humiliation and uses self-knowledge without changing his conduct. The pattern gives the novel a precise connection to online argument.

item.title: The 48-team World Cup drew record crowds and raised workload questions
item.description: FIFA reported 6.8 million spectators and 308 goals across 104 matches. Independent data on travel, player recovery and public cost will determine the fuller case for expansion.
